hypothesis: Adding a PHP lint gate (php -l on all changed .php files) before any commit will catch syntax errors that currently reach CI and fail the smoke test. Current surface has no pre-push local validation beyond manual review. Hypothesis: php -l pre-commit check eliminates class of syntax-error CI failures, improving first-push pass rate.

Extending the pre-push smoke test from HTTP-200+no-PHP-error to also assert presence of structural HTML/CSS class markers (.site-header, .site-footer, main.site-main) in page responses will catch layout-structure regressions before CI. The 2 Playwright layout failures (footer-v2 grid-template-areas regression, homepage-parity banner) represent the highest remaining addressable failure class post-php-lint-gate. A grep-for-marker smoke pass on changed templates would have caught both before push.

The current structural marker smoke test hits only 3 URLs (homepage, /products/, /services/). A single-product template regression or category archive collapse would pass the gate undetected. Expanding the smoke URL set from 3 to 6 — adding a live single-product URL, a service-detail URL, and a product category archive URL — will catch template-specific regressions on a broader set of WP template files before they reach CI. Basis: both prior keeps (php -l, marker check) targeted identified failure classes with surgical gates; this extends the proven marker-check approach to template types that appeared in the historical failure record (product taxonomy seed failure mapped to product archive templates; homepage-parity banner mapped to homepage template). Adding 3 representative pages triples per-push template coverage at negligible curl cost.
